name: Chicken in Coconut Milk Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 quarter-sized pieces dried galangal, also known as Siamese Ginger (or twice as much fresh galangal)
4 stalks fresh lemon grass, bruised and cut into 2-inch lengths
1 large whole boned chicken, breast cut into half-inch pieces (about 1 lb, boned)
3-4 fresh serrano chiles, sliced into rounds
recipeInstructions:
In Thai, this is called Gai Tom Kha. Be sure to use unsweetened coconut milk, which is available in Asian markets.
1. In a saucepan, bring the coconut milk, chicken stock, galangal and lemon grass to a boil.
2. Reduce heat and simmer until the mixture begins to thicken.
3. Strain the stock through a fine mesh sieve into another pan or pot, discarding the galangal and lemon grass.
4. Return the strained stock to a boil in the same pan, then add the chicken pieces and reduce the heat to a simmer for about 2 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through.
5. Stir in fish sauce, kaffir lime leaves, serrano chiles, and lime juice.
6. Adjust seasoning with more chili rounds or coriander leaves if desired.
7. Serve hot.
